channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Security in React, React Compiler Rust Port, Server Functions in parallel, Auth book, Vercel Sandbox Docker Support
item.description: Security in React Applications A thorough guide to securing React apps beyond the built-in protections. Covers how to prevent XSS, sanitize HTML with DOMPurify, store auth tokens safely, validate...

item.title: usePagination: 3 lines of code, infinite possibilities
item.description: Paginated lists are one of the most common patterns in frontend development. A hand-written implementation — with state management for page, pageSize, total, loading, error, and handlers for...

item.title: The One Thing That Actually Got Me Through FAANG Interviews
item.description: The One Thing That Actually Got Me Through FAANG Interviews Quick context (why you're writing this) I remember staring at my screen at 2 a.m., third attempt at a medium‑level LeetCode problem,...

item.title: LLM-Free Multi-Agent Memory Architecture: How to Build Real Team Memory with Jira + GitHub + Commit Log
item.description: Introduction One of the biggest problems in software teams is not writing code. Code eventually gets written, refactored, tested, and deployed. The real challenge, most of the time, is this: "Why...

item.title: GPT-3.5-Turbo drops from 90% accuracy to 50% when the answer sits in the middle of a 20k-token prompt instead of the sta
item.description: GPT-3.5-Turbo drops from 90% accuracy to 50% when the answer sits in the middle of a 20k-token prompt instead of the start or end. Liu et al. (2023) documented this in "Lost in the Middle: How...

item.title: THORChain $10.7M Proposer-Forgery Attack

item.title: I Ran My Business With AI for 30 Days: Results and Lessons
item.description: In February I handed my freelance business to an AI assistant. Not "used AI to help" — actually handed it over. Email responses, client communication, code reviews, architectural decisions,...

item.title: Real-Time Monitoring for AI Agents: Beyond Log Streaming
item.description: Most agent monitoring is "log everything and grep later." That's not monitoring — that's archaeology. What We Actually Need Live execution view — Which agent is running right now? State inspection...

item.title: Porsche’s Cayenne Coupe Turbo will even make 911 owners nervous
item.description: Back in 2002, Porsche fans sputtered with rage as the Cayenne made its debut at the Paris Motor. More than 20 years later, Porsche now sells more SUVs than anything else in its lineup. Last year,...

item.title: OpenAI and Anthropic May Be Rivals, but Investors Aren’t Picking Sides
item.description: “Why wouldn’t you want to be in both Pepsi and Coke?” says one venture capitalist. “It’s the same here.”

item.title: Why Apple Might Put Cameras Into Its Next AirPods
item.description: From battery life to privacy, there are many hurdles to the idea taking off.

item.title: Are AI chatbots making us lose control of our brains?
item.description: This week I’ve been at SXSW London. There’s been music, film, and a lot—and I mean a lot—of talk about AI. I also had the opportunity to sit down with Gloria Mark, a psychologist at the University...

item.title: The Meta hack shows there’s more to AI security than Mythos
item.description: On June 5, 404 Media reported that attackers had been using Meta’s AI customer support agent to steal Instagram accounts. Their approach was simple: They asked the agent to link the accounts to...

item.title: AI Has Come for Serif Fonts
item.description: AI companies are using serif to project humanity. Critics are calling it “tasteslop.”

item.title: Hackers Exploit Critical Everest Forms Pro WordPress Plugin Flaw to Take Over Sites
item.description: Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical security flaw in Everest Forms Pro, a WordPress plugin with about 4,000 active installations, to execute arbitrary code, leading to a complete site...

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Scams Are Already Live: Fake Sites, Banking Malware, and Stolen Logins
item.description: Security researchers and the FBI are warning that a wave of FIFA-themed fraud is already hitting World Cup 2026 fans, days before the June 11 kickoff. Recent reports describe thousands of...

item.title: PCPJack Hijacks 230 AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure Servers for Covert SMTP Relay Network
item.description: The threat actor known as PCPJack has hijacked cloud servers associated with Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure to create a covert SMTP email relay network. "Compromised...

item.title: Mira Murati steps back into the spotlight, carefully
item.description: In the current environment, remaining heads down has diminishing returns; at some point, you have to make some noise just to remind the market you exist.

item.title: Valve says it’s ready to launch the Steam Machine this summer
item.description: Valve now says that the delayed Steam Machine PC and Steam Frame VR headset are set to launch sometime this summer. In a Thursday blog post detailing its Verified programs for both pieces of...

item.title: Ahead of its IPO, Anthropic’s Daniela Amodei shrugs off doubts about AI’s returns
item.description: Anthropic has been growing at a breakneck pace. The company announced that annualized revenue crossed $47 billion in May, up dramatically from roughly $9 billion at the end of 2025. That...

item.title: Airbnb’s Brian Chesky plans to launch a new AI lab
item.description: The Airbnb CEO said last year it hasn't struck an LLM partnership because existing products weren't quite ready.

item.title: Defense tech, AI, and fundraising take center stage at StrictlyVC Los Angeles on June 18
item.description: On Thursday, June 18, at The Aerospace Corporation Campus, investors, founders, and tech leaders will gather for an evening of conversation exploring some of the most consequential shifts taking...

item.title: Cyberdecks used to look like little laptops, but now they’re getting more personal
item.description: DIYer and TikTok user Annike Tan, who goes by @ubeboobey, can carry her cyberdeck around without anyone noticing because it doesn't look like a computer at all. Tan, who has been featured in The...

item.title: Dashlane explains how attackers managed to download encrypted password vaults
item.description: By targeting large numbers of users, attackers increased their chances of success.

item.title: Meta steals a tactic from Tesla and builds data centers in tents
item.description: Meta may have found one way to slash its massive data center bill: tents.

item.title: Kevin O’Leary agrees to downsize massive Utah data center
item.description: Kevin O'Leary agreed to halve the size of his planned 40,000-acre data center in Utah amid mounting pressure from residents and activists, as reported earlier by local affiliate ABC4. The Shark...

item.title: Google is letting social media stars customize their search result page
item.description: Google now lets big creators and publishers in the US claim dedicated profiles in Search to highlight things like videos, articles, and their other profiles online. But this feature won't be...

item.title: Amazon’s new plan for games: James Bond and AI Snoop Dogg
item.description: Amazon's gaming strategy has never really been clear. It's been very active in the space: acquiring Twitch, launching its Luna cloud gaming service nearly six years ago, investing heavily in MMOs...
